name: Poem: "Special Hero" — Christine M. Kerschan

sourceRefs.label: civilserviceaspirants.in - dedicated MCQ set, figures of speech per line

researchNotes: OCR index search for 'special hero'/'kerschan' returned 1 hit, but it was a false positive (the title appeared only as a wrong-answer distractor option for a different, unrelated poem about a computer). Web research only for this topic. Raw pool of 6 sourced MCQs, all kept since each tests a distinct line/device.

reading.intro: A poem dedicated to the poet's father, calling him her 'special hero' for his love and protection. Real coaching-site evidence tests figure-of-speech identification per line — and uniquely among the poems covered so far, several DIFFERENT devices appear across just 6 short lines, so precision matters more than pattern-guessing here. Expansion pass (Batch 20): added the poem's overall warm/sincere tone and how its brevity concentrates its emotional impact.

qa.question: What is "Special Hero" about?
qa.answer: The poet's tribute to her father — recalling how he held her as a baby, gave her love and protection, and reflecting on how lucky she feels that he was 'chosen' to be her father.

qa.question: Identify the figure of speech: "There is something special about a father's love."
Identify the figure of speech: "Seems it was sent to me from someplace up above."
qa.answer: Hyperbole — an exaggerated, larger-than-literal claim (the love being literally 'sent from above') used for emotional emphasis, not meant literally.

qa.question: Identify the figure of speech: "How did I get so lucky, you were the dad chosen for me?"
qa.answer: Rhetorical question — asked for emotional effect, not expecting a literal answer.

qa.question: What figure of speech is the repetition of "I just wanted you to know"?
qa.answer: Anaphora — repetition of the same phrase at the start of successive lines.

qa.question: Identify the figure of speech: "Our love is everlasting."
qa.answer: Hyperbole — an absolute, exaggerated claim ('everlasting') used to emphasize depth of feeling.

qa.question: What overall tone does "Special Hero" convey, and how does its brevity (just 6 lines analysed) affect its emotional impact?
qa.answer: A warm, sincere, grateful tone — the poem's short length concentrates its emotional weight into just a few direct statements, avoiding lengthy narrative in favour of an intense, personal, almost diary-entry-like tribute, which intensifies rather than dilutes its sentiment.

questions.prompt: "Special Hero" is dedicated to whom, and written by whom?
options: Dedicated to a friend, by Sarojini Naidu
Dedicated to her father, by Christine M. Kerschan
Dedicated to a teacher, by David Bates
Dedicated to a soldier, by Edgar Albert Guest
